item.title: Vercel Says Internal Systems Hit in Breach - Decipher
item.description: The incident came to light on Sunday and the company says it has brought in an incident response provider to investigate the intrusion. Details of the intrusion are scant at this point.

item.title: Vercel April 2026 security incident | Vercel Knowledge Base
item.description: We’ve identified a security incident that involved unauthorized access to certain internal Vercel systems.

item.title: Any Color You Like: NIST Scientists Create ‘Any Wavelength’ Lasers in Tiny Circuits for Light
item.description: NIST scientists and collaborators have pioneered a way to make integrated circuits for light by depositing complex patterns of specialized materials onto silicon wafers.

item.title: Thoughts and Feelings around Claude Design · Sam Henri Gold

item.description: Table of contents for migrating ASP.NET ASMX web services to ASP.NET Core

item.title: Code Coverage Showdown: Microsoft's Code Coverage vs Coverlet | Pure Krome
item.description: Comparing Microsoft's Code Coverage tool with Coverlet for .NET projects with respect to line, branch, and method coverage.

item.title: The electromechanical angle computer inside the B-52 bomber's star tracker
item.description: Before GPS, how did aircraft navigate? One important technique was celestial navigation: navigating from the positions of the stars, planets...

item.title: Task Manager is LYING About Your CPU Usage (Here's the Truth)
item.description: Ever wonder why Task Manager shows a CPU value that doesn't "feel" right? Dave explains why Task Manager shows you the values it does and how they are determined. Comments with question marks get answered on our weekly ShopTalk: https://youtu.be/iT0T52OV9B8
item.author: (Dave's Garage)

item.title: Why Japan has such good railways - Works in Progress Magazine
item.description: Japan's railways are the finest in the world. Other countries can copy its formula.

item.title: Sorry, Mary
item.description: [Note to reader: This is a sequel to my widely-circulated 2021 essay “The Great Solar Bleed-Out Scam”; for the sake of readers who have forgotten or never read that earlier essay, I include it here…

item.title: State of Kdenlive - 2026
item.description: In 2025, the Kdenlive team continued grinding to push the project forward through steady development, collaboration, and community support. Over the past year we’ve found a nice balance between adding new features, bug fixing, polishing the user interface, and improving performance and workflow, with stability taking priority over feature creep.

item.title: The Listener
item.description: Private Knowledge

item.title: Building a Claude Code Skill Plugin from Scratch | Personal blog at franchb.com
item.description: A practical guide to building, structuring, versioning, and securing Claude Code skill plugins — lessons from creating fp-go-skill.

item.title: Alex L. Zhang | The Mismanaged Geniuses Hypothesis
item.description: We propose the mismanaged geniuses hypothesis, which posits that existing frontier language models are severely underutilized due to sub-optimal use of individual language model calls.

item.title: Tokenomics - Anthropic Token Cost Calculator
item.description: Compare how the same transcript is counted for requests to Anthropic Opus 4.6 versus Anthropic Opus 4.7

item.title: Just let me compute in peace
item.description: Computing can be so noisy these days

item.title: Audio transcription is worse in 2026 than it was in 2016
item.description: I remember in 2016 I could dictate whole novels with Dragon NaturallySpeaking, which at that time worked on my Mac. They no longer supp...

item.title: Fire Framework
item.description: 🔥 The FIRE Framework: One Mental Model to Answer 80% of Backend Interview Questions

item.title: Claude knows who you are
item.description: Claude Opus 4.7 is solidly superhuman at stylometric identification of authors.

item.title: DOOM runs in ChatGPT and Claude / Blog
item.description: How I built a playable DOOM MCP app that runs inline in compatible AI clients and falls back to a browser launch URL.

item.title: The Blast Radius Problem: Running Your Coding Agent in YOLO Mode
item.description: Running your coding agent in bypass mode on your laptop is the mistake. A VM with Tailscale and default-deny egress is how you shrink the blast radius.

item.title: SQLite prefixes its temp files with `etilqs_` - blag
item.description: Here’s some wild lore inside SQLite’s source code explaining why temp files start with etilqs_

item.title: Why LLMs Aren't Giving You the Result You Expect | Why I Prefer Claude Code Today
item.description: Every week somebody tells me 'I canceled my Claude plan, it just doesn't perform as well as GPT for me.' I've got 500+ hours in Claude Code and Codex, 400k lines generated, and both deliver. The difference isn't the model. It's how people are talking to it.
